title: Package Layout, Pyproject Metadata, Entry Points, and Reproducible Builds · Language Clinic: Src Layout
summary: Bookbot works only from the repository root, imports an uninstalled local file, lacks metadata and version identity, builds from a dirty tree, and publishes a wheel never tested after installation. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the src layout increment in installable reproducible Bookbot package. Learners…
objectives: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
Gate representative input slices on correctness first and practical time and memory thresholds second.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-src-layout — Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-packaging-release-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-packaging-release-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_packaging_release_3(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-src-layout-1lynlh
# Competency: bookbot-src-layout.
# Case 2795: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def evidence_bookbot_src_layout_1lynlh(metadata_fields: int = 9, artifact_count: int = 2, clean_revision: bool = True, contents_inspected: bool = True, fresh_install_passed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1lynlh = "1lynlh-hlnyl1-2795"
    assert evidence_variant_1lynlh
    evidence_axes_1lynlh = (
        "corpus1lynlh",
        "policyhlnyl1",
        "revision2795",
        "command1lynlh2795",
        "reporthlnyl12795",
        "test27951lynlh",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1lynlh)) == 6
    if metadata_fields < 6 or artifact_count < 2:
        return False, artifact_count, "package-shape"
    if not clean_revision:
        return False, artifact_count, "dirty-build"
    if not contents_inspected:
        return False, artifact_count, "unknown-contents"
    if not fresh_install_passed:
        return False, artifact_count, "install-failure"
    assert artifact_count >= 2
    return True, metadata_fields, "installable-artifacts"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Src Layout model · Theory Src Layout, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Src Layout precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Src Layout model · Theory Src Layout, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 9482 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build inspected wheel and source artifacts from an identified revision and test a fresh installation; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate import package, tests, fixtures, documentation, and build artifacts with a src layout and explicit package discovery.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Src Layout model · Theory Src Layout, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A src layout is decorative and cannot expose import mistakes.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Src Layout.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-src-layout-1lynlh.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-src-layout-1lynlh(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_src_layout_1lynlh\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?metadata_fields < 6)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not clean_revision)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not contents_inspected)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not fresh_install_passed)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using separate and import plus specific case evidence.
